nodes.name: sessions
nodes.summary: Initial migration creating the sessions table that tracks each user's onboarding stage, intake history, profile, and accumulated results, with supporting indexes on stage and update time.

nodes.name: tracked_competitors
nodes.summary: Migration creating the tracked_competitors table used to monitor competitor ad activity per user, storing the last seen ad IDs and indexed for due-for-check polling.

nodes.name: feedback_log
nodes.summary: Migration creating the feedback_log table that records user ratings and feedback text on generated skill outputs, indexed by skill/industry and by low ratings for quality review.

nodes.name: user_brand_voice
nodes.summary: Migration creating the user_brand_voice table that versions and stores each user's brand voice profile, with an active-version flag and an index for quick lookup by user.

nodes.name: user_campaign_history
nodes.summary: Migration enabling the pgvector extension and creating the user_campaign_history table, which stores past campaign briefs and an embedding column for similarity search across industry, stage, and competitor context.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the pgvector extension to add a vector embedding column with a dedicated similarity index, enabling semantic retrieval of past campaign history.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Large normalization migration that restructures the schema into six core tables (users, user_business_profile, user_sessions_slim, skill_runs, campaigns, posts) replacing earlier denormalized session data, with extensive indexing for active users, quotas, and skill/campaign lookups.
nodes.languageNotes: A large-scale normalization migration splitting a monolithic sessions table into six purpose-specific tables with foreign-key-style relations and 14 supporting indexes.

nodes.name: user_business_profile
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; stores each user's business profile attributes (industry, stage, target customer, revenue, budget, channels, competitors) split out from the original sessions table.

nodes.name: user_sessions_slim
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; a lean per-user session table tracking the currently selected task, intake history, and tone calibration, replacing the bulkier original sessions table.

nodes.name: skill_runs
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; logs each invocation of a marketing skill per user, including generated content, model used, and rating, indexed for recent and per-skill lookups.

nodes.name: campaigns
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; represents a marketing campaign per user with an offer lever, end date, embedding, and link back to the originating skill run, indexed for active-campaign queries.

nodes.name: posts
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; tracks individual scheduled content posts belonging to a campaign, with day, status, and link fields, indexed by user/channel and parent campaign.

nodes.name: engagements
nodes.summary: Migration introducing the engagement-centric data spine: creates engagements, diagnostic_briefs, and strategies tables to model a structured consulting workflow per user, replacing ad-hoc campaign tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: Introduces a hierarchical engagement -> diagnostic brief -> strategy data model with versioning and token-usage tracking, supported by 10 indexes.

nodes.name: diagnostic_briefs
nodes.summary: Part of the engagement spine migration; stores diagnostic brief documents generated for an engagement, tracking token usage and creation time.

nodes.name: strategies
nodes.summary: Part of the engagement spine migration; stores versioned marketing strategy documents tied to an engagement and diagnostic brief, including content pillars, KPI dashboard, model used, and user feedback rating.

nodes.name: user_sessions_slim (v2 fields)
nodes.summary: Small migration adding missing V2 columns (pending_followup_skill, content_outputs, token_log) to user_sessions_slim and a cost_used_usd column to users, fixing gaps from the V1-to-V2 session adapter cutover.

nodes.name: campaign_intelligence
nodes.summary: Migration creating the campaign_intelligence table that captures industry-specific campaign intelligence events per user, indexed by industry, event, and creation time.

nodes.name: user_fb_connections
nodes.summary: Migration creating Facebook integration tables: user_fb_connections for OAuth-linked ad accounts, oauth_states for the OAuth handshake, ads_snapshots for daily ad performance metrics, and ads_alert_cooldowns to throttle repeat alerts.

nodes.name: oauth_states
nodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; stores short-lived OAuth state tokens used to validate the Facebook OAuth callback flow per user.

nodes.name: ads_snapshots
nodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; stores daily snapshots of Facebook ad campaign performance metrics (impressions, reach, clicks, CTR, CPM, leads, purchases) per user, indexed by user and date.

nodes.name: ads_alert_cooldowns
nodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; tracks cooldown state per user/campaign/alert-type to prevent duplicate ad performance alerts from being sent repeatedly.

nodes.name: user_fb_connections (available_accounts)
nodes.summary: Tiny migration adding an available_accounts JSONB column to user_fb_connections so users can switch between Facebook ad accounts without re-authenticating via OAuth.
